package handler

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for no_streaming_for_large_export_handler: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Large list or export handlers that materialize everything before writing rather than using chunked or streaming output.
type CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerModel
}

type CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerService struct {
    repo *CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveNoStreamingForLargeExportHandler(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseNoStreamingForLargeExportHandlerDTO) error {
    var export strings.Builder
    export.WriteString("id,tenant_id,status,amount\n")
    for _, item := range items {
        _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(&export, "%s,%s,%s,%d\n", item.ID, item.TenantID, item.Status, item.Amount)
    }
    c.Data(http.StatusOK, "text/csv", []byte(export.String()))
    return nil
}
